_about: SITE-R register — every spck.dev site requirement as an enforceable row (design spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-website-ia-redesign-design.md). ADD-ONLY: removing or weakening a row requires a reasoned entry in site_requirements_retired.json; enforced by `site_gates.py tdd` against git HEAD. Test id conventions: 'site_smoke:<name>' = a test block tagged `// SITE-R-xxx` in tests/web/browser/site_smoke.mjs; 'gate:<mode>' = a site_gates.py mode; 'responsive_smoke' = the existing tests/web/browser/responsive_smoke.mjs suite. The tdd gate is GREEN only at 100% (every row has >=1 existing test, no orphan test tags).

requirements.requirement: Unified nav byte-identical on all 6 pages (spck · Merchants /check · Agents /agent · Coverage /coverage · Docs /docs · GitHub↗); contains no /tool link
No 'Launch Tool' text anywhere on the site
Exactly one .btn-primary above the fold per page
/check depth ladder sections in DOM order instant → cli → ci (data-ladder-step markers)
Unofficial-disclaimer sentence on every page (independent + unofficial + not affiliated + the official suite is authoritative)
OG + Twitter share meta on every page
CLASS-AUTO numbers are data-rendered: any number that exists in coverage/agent-coverage JSON carries data-live and its static fallback text equals the live JSON value at gate time
No unregistered factual claims: every claim candidate is LIVE-bound (data-live) or registered in public/site_claims.json with evidence and an unexpired review_by
Voice banned patterns absent (superlatives, achievement framing, 'certified'/'endorsed', unverifiable stats, third-party names outside the data-attribution block)
/tool → /check and /guide → /docs 301 redirects present in public/_redirects; no page links to /tool or /guide
Security: /* headers include nosniff, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP default-src 'self'; no un-esc()'d innerHTML/document.write/insertAdjacentHTML sinks; no external script/style/font origins
Sandbox loads >= 6 demo cases
Pages render meaningfully if the coverage JSON fetch fails (graceful degradation, no blank main)
Zero console errors on load of every page
No horizontal overflow at 320px (and 375px) on every page
Analytics beacons: home_view on /, page_view beacons on /check and /docs
Product-manifest change (merchant/agent check counts, defect count, spec versions) forces a site claims review before the lane goes green
Hook-first homepage: badge + H1 hook + exactly two hero CTAs (/check, /agent) above the fold

Shared design system: every page links /site.css and never redefines a shared component (.site-nav/.site-footer/.btn-primary/.btn-secondary/.btn-ghost) in inline <style> — the look is single-sourced so the nav/footer/buttons cannot re-theme per page

Admin access control: every /api/admin/* route rejects unauthenticated requests (401/403) and authenticated non-admin sessions (403); the admin allowlist is manageable ONLY by a super-admin (POST/DELETE /api/admin/admins); a super-admin can never be removed or demoted via the API; management actions are audit-logged

Admin page (public/admin.html): carries noindex,nofollow; is linked from no nav or public page; uses the standard shell (site.css, byte-identical nav, disclaimer footer); renders every metric from runtime API responses with no static numeric claims in markup

Page-view funnel: home/check/docs/coverage view and return beacon events are allow-listed in /api/track (a sent event must not be silently dropped) and each of those pages sends its view/return beacon

Save-report flow: a completed instant check offers an email+OTP save; saving requires an authenticated session (401 otherwise); the saved report is retrievable by its owner and walled from other users; each save records the store domain on the user (email-to-domain mapping) and attempts a best-effort report email that never blocks the save; the report_saved conversion beacon is allow-listed
Admin dashboard reflects the current product: the users listing exposes each user's recorded store domains (the email-to-domain lead mapping) and the funnel pane counts report_saved conversions

Public opt-in conformance badge: GET /api/badge/<reportId>.svg serves an SVG derived from the saved report's summary (grade, pass/total, date) with no authentication — the unguessable report id is shared only by its owner embedding the badge. Missing/expired ids return a 200 'unknown' badge so embedded images never break, every interpolated value is XML-escaped, and responses are publicly cacheable.
